prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. A protracted refugee emergency commenced in Africa in mid-April 2023, following the initiation of the 2023 Sudan conflict. As of May 1, 2023, approximately 73,000 to 100,000 individuals [1] had departed the country, concurrently with over 5 million people being internally displaced. This populace comprised diplomats, citizens, and foreign nationals hailing from various nations such as Somalia, Eritrea, Brazil, the United States, the United Kingdom, Kenya, and Uganda. Additionally, thousands more were reported to have been uprooted, predominantly inhabitants of Khartoum. On April 24, 2023, several nations, including Chad and South Sudan, confirmed the arrival of multiple thousands of civilians. Some of these individuals traveled by bus, car, or on foot, enduring extremely perilous circumstances. The overwhelming majority of these civilians sought refuge in the adjacent nation of Chad, with the United Nations indicating that most of these individuals originated from
B. An ongoing refugee crisis began in Africa in mid-April 2023 after the outbreak of the 2023 Sudan conflict. By 1 May 2023, an estimated 73,000 to 100,000 people [1] have fled the country, while over 5 million people have been displaced within the country. These included diplomats and citizens and foreign nationals from countries including Somalia, Eritrea, Brazil, the United States, the United Kingdom, Kenya and Uganda. Thousands more were reported to have been displaced, mainly residents of Khartoum. On 24 April 2023, several countries including Chad and South Sudan reported several thousand civilians, some coming by bus or car or on foot in severely dangerous conditions.The vast majority of these civilians entered the neighboring country of Chad with the UN reporting most of these people came from
C. Following the outbreak of the 2023 Sudan conflict, an ongoing refugee crisis began in Africa in mid-April 2023. By 1 May 2023, an estimated 73,000 to 100,000 people had fled the country, and over 5 million people had been displaced within the country. This included diplomats and citizens from countries like Somalia, Eritrea, Brazil, the United States, the United Kingdom, Kenya, and Uganda. Thousands more people, mostly residents of Khartoum, were displaced, with many arriving by bus or on foot in dangerous conditions.

Answer:

Some of these individuals traveled by bus, car, or on foot, enduring extremely perilous circumstances. The overwhelming majority of these civilians sought refuge in the adjacent nation of Chad, with the United Nations indicating that most of these individuals originated from
options.B: An ongoing refugee crisis began in Africa in mid-April 2023 after the outbreak of the 2023 Sudan conflict. By 1 May 2023, an estimated 73,000 to 100,000 people [1] have fled the country, while over 5 million people have been displaced within the country. These included diplomats and citizens and foreign nationals from countries including Somalia, Eritrea, Brazil, the United States, the United Kingdom, Kenya and Uganda. Thousands more were reported to have been displaced, mainly residents of Khartoum. options.C: Following the outbreak of the 2023 Sudan conflict, an ongoing refugee crisis began in Africa in mid-April 2023. By 1 May 2023, an estimated 73,000 to 100,000 people had fled the country, and over 5 million people had been displaced within the country. This included diplomats and citizens from countries like Somalia, Eritrea, Brazil, the United States, the United Kingdom, Kenya, and Uganda.
